items.title: ‘Day-to-day dread’ haunts frustrated job seekers in era of low hiring. When will it end?
items.description: Finding a job was easy a few years ago. Now, Americans are worried about their job prospects, even though the unemployment rate is historically low.

items.title: Regular water is no longer enough. Welcome to the corporate gold rush around ‘functional hydration.’
items.description: Social media and GLP-1s are spurring increased interest in hydration products, and companies are responding with things like electrolyte mixes and “hydrating hot chocolate.”

items.title: The U.S. stock market is becoming ‘too big to fail’
items.description: Why protracted bear markets may be a thing of the past.

items.title: Fed Chair Warsh will be in the hot seat as lawmakers press for his read on the economy
items.description: Kevin Warsh will testify before Congress for the first time as Fed chair this week. Lawmakers will want answers.

items.title: ‘It’s heartbreaking’: My brother claimed Social Security at 70. He died from cancer after one payment. Why wait to claim?
items.description: “I’ve always been a little skeptical of the government’s encouragement to delay claiming benefits.”

items.title: Apple sues OpenAI for alleged theft of confidential info — and says that’s just ‘the tip of the iceberg’
items.description: The iPhone maker claims “misconduct is normalized and exemplified by leadership” at OpenAI.

items.title: Meta’s stock roars back to life as it notches its best week in years
items.description: Investors are bullish on Meta’s new low-cost AI pricing and infrastructure plans.

items.title: SK Hynix’s stock sees double-digit pop in Nasdaq debut
items.description: The South Korean memory-chip company’s new American depositary receipts began trading on Friday and logged gains of nearly 13%.

items.title: ‘I’d hate to end up with an unexpected tax bill’: I’m 73 and still work full time. Can I avoid paying taxes on my Social Security benefits?
items.description: “I’m actually earning more each week than I ever have before.”

items.title: ‘I get $1,460 in Social Security’: My millionaire ex-husband, 74, refuses to pay alimony. What can I do?
items.description: “I have very little money, while my ex’s financial statements show assets in the millions.”
